sources.title: Migrate content from .mini-code-agent/ to allowed project paths
sources.summary: review source: .mini-code-agent/ directory was created outside allowed paths, causing a scope violation block.
evidence_snippets: .mini-code-agent/ directory was created outside allowed paths, causing a scope violation block.

sources.title: Add minimal reviewer checks for task completion
sources.summary: A conservative reviewer pass is needed before a run is usable.
evidence_snippets: A conservative reviewer pass is needed before a run is usable.

sources.title: Capture git diff and test result
sources.summary: A code agent run is not reviewable unless it records diff and tests.
evidence_snippets: A code agent run is not reviewable unless it records diff and tests.

sources.title: Implement shell command tool with allowlist
sources.summary: Coding agents need shell access bounded by an explicit local safety policy.
evidence_snippets: Coding agents need shell access bounded by an explicit local safety policy.

sources.title: Phase 4 source: minimal agent loop
sources.summary: Minimal agent loop and evidence requirements for AI builders.
evidence_snippets: Minimal agents should keep a simple loop: model proposes an action, the runtime executes a bounded tool, the observation returns to the model, and the run records trajectory, diff, tests, and review evidence for the builder. A useful AI bui

sources.title: Add runtime executor for bounded tools
sources.summary: The agent loop needs a bounded tool executor that runs actions (read, write, bash) within safety boundaries.
evidence_snippets: The agent loop needs a bounded tool executor that runs actions (read, write, bash) within safety boundaries.

sources.title: Implement agent loop: prompt -> action -> observation -> repeat
sources.summary: The core agent behavior is the action/observation loop extracted from references.
evidence_snippets: The core agent behavior is the action/observation loop extracted from references.

sources.title: minimal-agent 博客
sources.summary: 真实 code agent 产品需要把 trajectory、动作、观察、测试和 review 作为一等证据，而不是只展示 planner 文本。
evidence_snippets: 真实 code agent 产品需要把 trajectory、动作、观察、测试和 review 作为一等证据，而不是只展示 planner 文本。

sources.title: Define tool protocol and model action schema
sources.summary: Reference code-agent projects converge on typed action and observation contracts.
evidence_snippets: Reference code-agent projects converge on typed action and observation contracts.

sources.title: Improve test coverage for LLM backlog and runtime modules
sources.summary: review source: Changed files included `tests/test_llm_backlog.py` and `tests/test_llm_runtime.py` but the execution was blocked before tests ran. Ensure these tests are robust and cover edge cases.
evidence_snippets: Changed files included `tests/test_llm_backlog.py` and `tests/test_llm_runtime.py` but the execution was blocked before tests ran. Ensure these tests are robust and cover edge cases.

sources.title: Paper Note - Evidence-Backed Agent Workflows
sources.summary: paper source: # Paper Note - Evidence-Backed Agent Workflows ## Claim Agent workflows become more reliable when each action is grounded in explicit evidence, a clear acceptance criterion, and a bounded tool-use policy. ## Method The note compares agent loops that act from h
evidence_snippets: Agent workflows become more reliable when each action is grounded in explicit
evidence, a clear acceptance criterion, and a bounded tool-use policy.
The note compares agent loops that act from hidden chain-of-thought with loops
that produce durable task packets, evidence snippets, and reviewable execution
records.

Supports custom ASCII species — bring your own Pikachu! ![Custom Pikachu buddy companion](assets/buddy-pikachu.jpg) [Full Buddy docs →](docs/buddy.md) --- ## Features ### Core - **Interactive REPL** with streaming output, command history, slash command autocomplete - **Agentic tool loop** — Claude calls tools autonomously until the task is complete - **9 built-in tools**: `Read`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash`, `AskUser`, `EnterPlanMode`, `ExitPlanMode` - **Plan mode** — parallel subagents explore codebase before you implement, with permission isolation - **Permission system** — mode-aware (default/plan), reads auto-approved, writes/bash ask for confirmation - **Session persistence** — auto-save conversations, `/resume` to continue later - **Context compression** — auto-compact when approaching token limits - **

tickets.title: Resolve external execution blocker
tickets.summary: All previous executions are blocked because ARIADNE_ENABLE_EXTERNAL_EXECUTION is not set. This must be fixed to proceed with any code tasks.
acceptance_criteria: ARIADNE_ENABLE_EXTERNAL_EXECUTION environment variable is set to 1 in all relevant contexts.
A manual test confirms that a simple code task (e.g., write a file) executes successfully.

tickets.title: Implement local execution safety permissions
tickets.summary: No explicit safety model exists; permissions for read, write, and bash operations must be mode-aware to prevent unintended actions.
acceptance_criteria: Read operations are auto-approved.
Write and bash operations require explicit confirmation before execution.
Unit tests cover both auto-approve and confirmation scenarios.

tickets.title: Enhance CLI with session persistence
tickets.summary: The CLI must persist session or trajectory data for review and debugging, as required by the test-first architecture.
acceptance_criteria: CLI accepts a --session-dir flag to specify where trajectory logs are stored.
Each command invocation appends a structured log entry (timestamp, action, observation) to a file in the session directory.
Tests verify that the log file is created and contains the expected entries.

tickets.title: Add runtime executor for bounded tools
tickets.summary: The agent loop needs a bounded tool executor that runs actions (read, write, bash) within safety boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Executor can run a read-file tool and return file contents.
Executor can run a write-file tool after safety approval.
Executor can run a bash command after safety approval.
All tool results are returned as structured observations.

tickets.title: Add observation collector
tickets.summary: Observations from tool executions must be collected and structured for the agent loop and evidence recording.
acceptance_criteria: Collector receives tool outputs and converts them into a standard Observation object.
Observation includes fields: tool_name, status, result, metadata.
Collector can handle errors and timeouts gracefully.

tickets.title: Add review checker with conservative pass
tickets.summary: A reviewer pass is needed before a run is considered usable; this ensures quality and safety.
acceptance_criteria: Checker evaluates a run's trajectory and produces a verdict (pass/fail/blocked).
Conservative rules: any unapproved write or bash action causes a fail.
Checker returns structured evidence including list of issues.

tickets.title: Implement minimal agent loop
tickets.summary: The core agent loop drives the cycle: model proposes action, runtime executes, observation returns, and the loop continues.
acceptance_criteria: Loop takes a goal string and a maximum step count.
For each step, it invokes the LLM client to get an action, runs it via the executor, collects observation, and checks termination.
Trajectory of all steps is saved for review.

tickets.title: Implement issue generation and scoping
tickets.summary: The Ariadne workbench requires issue generation with evidence references, acceptance criteria, and proper scoping to the target project.
acceptance_criteria: Generate a new issue from a theme, including evidence refs and affected modules.
Scoped to the current target project and version.
Historical tickets from same repo are excluded if not in the selected version.

tickets.title: Write comprehensive tests
tickets.summary: Tests must be a first-class citizen; every module should have corresponding tests in the tests/ directory.
acceptance_criteria: Tests exist for safety module, CLI, executor, collector, checker, and agent loop.
All tests pass with no external dependencies (mocked tools).
Test coverage is above 80% for core modules.

tickets.title: Integrate safety into agent loop
tickets.summary: The agent loop must call the safety module before executing any write or bash action to ensure permissions are respected.
acceptance_criteria: Before any write or bash action, the loop invokes the safety approval system.
If approval is denied, the action is skipped and the observation reports the denial.
Trajectory logs include safety decision details.

delivery_items.title: Resolve external execution blocker
acceptance_criteria: ARIADNE_ENABLE_EXTERNAL_EXECUTION environment variable is set to 1 in all relevant contexts.
A manual test confirms that a simple code task (e.g., write a file) executes successfully.

delivery_items.title: Implement local execution safety permissions
acceptance_criteria: Read operations are auto-approved.
Write and bash operations require explicit confirmation before execution.
Unit tests cover both auto-approve and confirmation scenarios.

delivery_items.title: Enhance CLI with session persistence
acceptance_criteria: CLI accepts a --session-dir flag to specify where trajectory logs are stored.
Each command invocation appends a structured log entry (timestamp, action, observation) to a file in the session directory.
Tests verify that the log file is created and contains the expected entries.

delivery_items.title: Add runtime executor for bounded tools
acceptance_criteria: Executor can run a read-file tool and return file contents.
Executor can run a write-file tool after safety approval.
Executor can run a bash command after safety approval.
All tool results are returned as structured observations.

delivery_items.title: Add observation collector
acceptance_criteria: Collector receives tool outputs and converts them into a standard Observation object.
Observation includes fields: tool_name, status, result, metadata.
Collector can handle errors and timeouts gracefully.

delivery_items.title: Add review checker with conservative pass
acceptance_criteria: Checker evaluates a run's trajectory and produces a verdict (pass/fail/blocked).
Conservative rules: any unapproved write or bash action causes a fail.
Checker returns structured evidence including list of issues.

delivery_items.title: Implement minimal agent loop
acceptance_criteria: Loop takes a goal string and a maximum step count.
For each step, it invokes the LLM client to get an action, runs it via the executor, collects observation, and checks termination.
Trajectory of all steps is saved for review.

delivery_items.title: Implement issue generation and scoping
acceptance_criteria: Generate a new issue from a theme, including evidence refs and affected modules.
Scoped to the current target project and version.
Historical tickets from same repo are excluded if not in the selected version.

delivery_items.title: Write comprehensive tests
acceptance_criteria: Tests exist for safety module, CLI, executor, collector, checker, and agent loop.
All tests pass with no external dependencies (mocked tools).
Test coverage is above 80% for core modules.

delivery_items.title: Integrate safety into agent loop
acceptance_criteria: Before any write or bash action, the loop invokes the safety approval system.
If approval is denied, the action is skipped and the observation reports the denial.
Trajectory logs include safety decision details.

这是一个参考项目：
# cc-mini **Ultra-light Harness scaffolding for AI agents** **Agentic**  ·  **Built to Extend**  ·  **From Claude Code**
The entire core is `~1000 lines of Python`
--- ### **NEW: Buddy — AI Companion with Custom Sprites** > Your coding companion lives in the terminal. Type `/buddy` to hatch it. Supports custom ASCII species — bring your own Pikachu! ![Custom Pikachu buddy companion](assets/buddy-pikachu.jpg) [Full Buddy docs →](docs/buddy.md) --- ## Features ### Core - **Interactive REPL** with streaming output, command history, slash command autocomplete - **Agentic tool loop** — Claude calls tools autonomously until the task is complete - **9 built-in tools**: `Read`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash`, `AskUser`, `EnterPlanMode`, `ExitPlanMode` - **Plan mode** — parallel subagents explore codebase before you implement, with permission isolation - **Permission system** — mode-aware (default/plan), reads auto-approved, writes/bash ask for confirmation - **Session persistence** — auto-save conversations, `/resume` to continue later - **Context compression** — auto-compact when approaching token limits - **

what_ariadne_understood:
这是一个参考项目：
# The minimal AI software engineering agent 📣 [mini-swe-agent now powers Ramp SWE-Bench](https://labs.ramp.com/swebench)
📣 [mini-swe-agent beats Claude Code and Codex on DeepSWE](https://deepswe.datacurve.ai/blog#evaluation-harness)
📣 [Run mini-swe-agent on our new & extremely challenging benchmark, ProgramBench](https://mini-swe-agent.com/latest/usage/programbench/)
📣 [New tutorial on building minimal AI agents](https://minimal-agent.com/) [![Docs](https://img.shields.io/badge/Docs-green?style=for-the-badge&logo=materialformkdocs&logoColor=white)](https://mini-swe-agent.com/latest/) [![Slack](https://img.shields.io/badge/Slack-4A154B?style=for-the-badge&logo=slack&logoColor=white)](https://join.slack.com/t/swe-bench/shared_invite/zt-36pj9bu5s-o3_yXPZbaH2wVnxnss1EkQ) [![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/mini-swe-agent?style=for-the-badge&logo=python&logoColor=white&labelColor=black&color=deeppink)](https://pypi.org/project/mini
已缓存 commit 674e908f。
已读取 README / manifest: pyproject.toml / tests: 57
可参考模式：Expose a small CLI as the primary builder interface.; Keep an executable test path for every agent iteration.
避免事项：Do not copy implementation files directly from the reference project.

artifacts.summary:
# The minimal AI software engineering agent 📣 [mini-swe-agent now powers Ramp SWE-Bench](https://labs.ramp.com/swebench)
📣 [mini-swe-agent beats Claude Code and Codex on DeepSWE](https://deepswe.datacurve.ai/blog#evaluation-harness)
📣 [Run mini-swe-agent on our new & extremely challenging benchmark, ProgramBench](https://mini-swe-agent.com/latest/usage/programbench/)
📣 [New tutorial on building minimal AI agents](https://minimal-agent.com/) [![Docs](https://img.shields.io/badge/Docs-green?style=for-the-badge&logo=materialformkdocs&logoColor=white)](https://mini-swe-agent.com/latest/) [![Slack](https://img.shields.io/badge/Slack-4A154B?style=for-the-badge&logo=slack&logoColor=white)](https://join.slack.com/t/swe-bench/shared_invite/zt-36pj9bu5s-o3_yXPZbaH2wVnxnss1EkQ) [![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/mini-swe-agent?style=for-the-badge&logo=python&logoColor=white&labelColor=black&color=deeppink)](https://pypi.org/project/mini

entrypoints: "litellm >= 1.75.5, != 1.82.7, != 1.82.8", # Security: Skip compromised 1.82.7/8; requires 1.75.5+ for GPT-5
avoid_notes: Do not copy implementation files directly from the reference project.
architecture_insights: Executable entrypoints detected: "Bug Tracker" = "https://github.com/SWE-agent/mini-swe-agent/issues", "litellm >= 1.75.5, != 1.82.7, != 1.82.8", # Security: Skip compromised 1.82.7/8; requires 1.75.5+ for GPT-5, "openai != 1.100.0,!=1.100.1",  # https://github.com/SWE-agent/mini-swe-agent/issues/446, Documentation = "https://mini-swe-agent.com/latest/".
Repository separates product code into a source/app package boundary.
Repository keeps tests as a first-class top-level module.
Repository language suggests an agent/runtime/tool boundary worth reflecting in issues.
Python package metadata is present, so CLI/package setup can be inferred.
safety_model: No explicit safety model was detected; generated issues should add local execution boundaries.
